title: Dark Horse Comics

|parent=*[[Wikipedia:Embracer Group|Embracer Group]]<ref name="Embracer">{{DarkHorse|url=Blog/3461/embracer-group-successfully-acquires-dark-horse-me|text=Embracer Group Successfully Acquires Dark Horse Media}}</ref>

{{Quote|Our goal was to create sequels and prequels to the films we loved, paying careful attention to quality and detail, essentially treating those films as though they were our own. ''Star Wars'' has been the crown jewel of this approach.|[[Mike Richardson]], on the end of Dark Horse's initial ''Star Wars'' publishing run|<ref name="Insider 151">[[Star Wars Insider 151|''Star Wars Insider'' 151]]</ref>}}
'''Dark Horse Comics''' is a publisher of [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] that over the years has produced many licensed works, a large number of which take place in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe.

==History==
Dark Horse began publishing comics in [[1986]] and soon it published titles tied to film properties, such as [[Wikipedia:Alien (franchise)|''Alien'']]/[[Wikipedia:Predator (franchise)|''Predator'']], [[Wikipedia:Terminator (franchise)|''Terminator'']] and others. It first published for ''Star Wars'' with ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' in [[1991]],<ref name="Timeline">{{DarkHorse|url=Company/Timeline|text=Timeline}}</ref> taking over from the earlier [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'' comics]]. While Dark Horse artists took liberties and deviated from other original properties, ''Star Wars'' demanded continuity; drawn characters looked like their respective actors, and ships and droids looked like their movie models.<ref name="secrets">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''</ref>

Unlike the "rudderless days" of [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]], with comics created without influence from [[Lucasfilm]], Dark Horse titles were tied directly with the rest of the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], and developed a creative dialogue with Lucasfilm both for ''Star Wars'' and [[Indiana Jones (franchise)|''Indiana Jones'']].<ref name="secrets" />

It republished and produced several compilations of works, including the aforementioned Marvel works, in a collection of trade paperbacks known as [[Star Wars Omnibus|''Star Wars'' Omnibus]]. The Omnibus imprint was also used for other fictional universes, such as ''Indiana Jones'' and [[Wikipedia:Buffy the Vampire Slayer|''Buffy the Vampire Slayer'']]. Dark Horse has a division called Dark Horse Digital, which allows customers to view comics online.

On January 3, [[2014]], [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] announced that the ''Star Wars'' comics license would move from Dark Horse back to Marvel in 2015.<ref name="Marvel">{{SW|url=news/lucasfilm-and-marvel-join-forces-to-publish-star-wars-comics-and-graphic-novels|text=Disney's Lucasfilm and Marvel Entertainment Join Forces to Publish ''Star Wars'' Comics and Graphic Novels}}</ref> After a six-year hiatus, it was announced on [[November 18]], [[2021]], that Dark Horse would return to publishing ''Star Wars'' comics alongside Marvel in [[2022]]. Multiple series are planned for the new program, with initial titles being part of the ''[[Star Wars: The High Republic]]'' publishing initiative<ref name="Return">{{SW|url=news/dark-horse-comics-announce|text=Dark Horse Comics Returns to the ''Star Wars'' Galaxy}}</ref> published in the spring of 2022. This re-launch of the Dark Horse ''Star Wars'' brand also includes one-shots, graphic novels, anthologies, and more.<ref name="Best">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-best-of-2021|text=''Star Wars'': Best of 2021}}</ref>
